matches.snippet: Tri Paisdinernes Eventyr Med Lochlanner Fathach I Fiounnisgehaven

matches.snippet: that the ear of Fionn Earwicker aforetime was the trademark of a broadcaster

matches.snippet: Way for Wet Week Welikins Douchka Marianne; The Last of the Fingallians; It Was

matches.snippet: Ophelia!'s Culpreints, Hear Hubty Hublin, My Old Dansh, I am Older northe Rogues among Whisht I Slips and He Calls Me his Dual of Ayessha, Suppotes a Ventriliquorst Merries a Corpse, Lapps for Finns This Funnycoons Week
matches.reasoning: Finns appears in catalogue of exotic/mythic peoples; phonetic proximity to Finn

matches.matched_text: The Fin had a flux and his Ehha a ride
matches.snippet: At Island Bridge she met her tide. Attahom, attahom, attahomhomhoom! The Fin had a flux and his Ehha a ride.
matches.reasoning: 'The Fin' = direct reference to the hero; 'flux' suggests bodily evacuation/fall motif; Ehha = variant of Ewa (ALP)

matches.matched_text: she who shuttered him after his fall
matches.snippet: cadet and prim, the hungray and anngreen...she who shuttered him after his fall and waked him widowt sparing
matches.reasoning: Fall motif linked to female figure's redemptive acts; wake/resurrection sequence

matches.snippet: she who shuttered him after his fall and waked him widowt sparing and gave him keen
matches.reasoning: Waking the corpse; parallel to Finnegan's wake where whiskey revives; part of resurrection cycle

matches.snippet: waked him widowt sparing and gave him keen and made him able
matches.reasoning: Keen = Irish funeral lament; giving/making 'keen' suggests ritual wake mourning

matches.snippet: hiding the crumbends of his enormousness in the areyou looking-for Pearlfar sea
